title: United Mizrahi Bank v Migdal Cooperative Village
source_name: United Mizrahi Bank v Migdal Cooperative Village
text:
Title: United Mizrahi Bank v Migdal Cooperative Village
Citation: CA 6821/93
Jurisdiction: israel
Source URL: https://versa.cardozo.yu.edu/

The Supreme Court treated Basic Laws on rights as having constitutional status and recognized judicial review of ordinary legislation inconsistent with protected rights. The decision is central to Israel's constitutional revolution and proportionality review.

title: Public Committee Against Torture in Israel v Government of Israel
source_name: Public Committee Against Torture in Israel v Government of Israel
text:
Title: Public Committee Against Torture in Israel v Government of Israel
Citation: HCJ 5100/94
Jurisdiction: israel
Source URL: https://versa.cardozo.yu.edu/

The High Court held that the security service lacked statutory authority to use physical interrogation methods amounting to coercion. The case is central to administrative legality, human dignity, security powers, and limits on necessity arguments.

title: Beit Sourik Village Council v Government of Israel
source_name: Beit Sourik Village Council v Government of Israel
text:
Title: Beit Sourik Village Council v Government of Israel
Citation: HCJ 2056/04
Jurisdiction: israel
Source URL: https://versa.cardozo.yu.edu/

The Court applied proportionality review to the route of the separation barrier, balancing security needs against harm to local residents. The case is important for military commander powers, occupation law, proportionality, and judicial review in security contexts.

title: Ka'adan v Israel Lands Administration
text:
Title: Ka'adan v Israel Lands Administration
Citation: HCJ 6698/95
Jurisdiction: israel
Source URL: https://versa.cardozo.yu.edu/

The Court held that the state could not allocate land in a manner that discriminated against Arab citizens. Ka'adan is a leading equality decision concerning public resources, state land, and constitutional principles of equal treatment.

title: Ressler v Knesset
text:
Title: Ressler v Knesset
Citation: HCJ 6298/07
Jurisdiction: israel
Source URL: https://versa.cardozo.yu.edu/

The Court addressed equality and military service exemptions for yeshiva students, invalidating legislative arrangements after constitutional proportionality analysis. The case is relevant to equality, religion-state relations, and judicial review of Basic Law rights.
